description_long: This is a vintage computer keyboard integrated into a single unit, featuring a full set of tactile keys in a rectangular, slightly sloped chassis. The casing is made of brownish plastic with ribbed vent-like grooves along the top and sides, and it includes a matching external power supply unit. The front face displays a classic key layout with function keys on the right and a prominent space bar. The rear panel is equipped with multiple ports, including audio jacks, cartridge slots, and serial connectors, indicating its use as a home computer. The side views reveal additional venting and a round power input port. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible seams and molded details typical of 1980s consumer electronics.
description: A vintage brown plastic computer keyboard with integrated ports and a matching external power supply, featuring a full key layout and ribbed vent details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keyboard layout, function keys, and the separate power supply unit.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the sloped profile, ribbed venting, and visible side ports.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying multiple input/output ports, including audio, cartridge, and serial connectors, as well as the back of the power supply.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the ribbed venting, round power input, and the side profile of both the keyboard and power supply.
attribute_key_layout: Full-size with function keys, no numpad

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
